name: SAPHO SYNDROME
definition: Synovitis, Acne, Palmoplantar pustulosis, Hyperostosis, Osteitis PUSTULOTIC ARTHROSTEITIS = STERNOCLAVICULAR HYPEROSTOSIS association of sterile osteoarticular inflammation with skin abnormalities (palmoplantar

demographics.Etiology: ? variant of psoriasis, exaggerated immune response in genetically susceptible
demographics.Histo: osteolytic portion of bone lesion contains plasma cells
demographics.Location: distal femur, proximal tibia, fibula, humerus, radius, ulna, rib

general: bone sclerosis combined with variable degree of osteolysis and periostitis:

endplate irregularities + CHARACTERISTIC anterior vertebral body corner erosion at

"bull's head" sign = increased radiotracer uptake in manubrium + both sternoclavicular
Axial skeleton: homogeneous osteosclerosis / osteolysis of one / more vertebral bodies (usually

Joints: joint narrowing, marginal erosion, hyperostosis, enthesopathy (later)

clinical.Prognosis: chronic course with unpredictable exacerbations + remissions
clinical.Rx: nonsteroidal antiinflammatory drugs, corticosteroids, methotrexate, tumor necrosis factor α
